sc_meta.purpose: Behavior contract for AI assistants operating in this repo via VS Code
sc_meta.inheritance_note: This file inherits persona, documentation/changelog discipline, and audit rules from the parent. It does not need to restate them — only project-specific detail or explicit overrides belong here.
sc_meta.update_rule: Any change to the rules below MUST update last_updated to the current ISO-8601 timestamp in the same commit.

always_reference.on_missing: If README.md or ROADMAP.md do not exist, ask before generating them — do not silently invent project intent.
always_reference.rule: Before proposing or writing code, re-check these files exist and are current in this session's context. Do not rely on a stale read from earlier in the conversation if either file could have changed.

investigation_policy.rule: Before suggesting new code, search the repo for existing related functions, modules, or patterns that already solve or partially solve the problem. Prefer extending/reusing over duplicating. State what was searched and what was found (or 'none found') before proposing new code.

targets: ROADMAP.md (mark items done/in-progress, do not delete history — strike through or move to a 'Completed' section)
any docs/ folder pages affected by the change
documentation_policy.rule: Documentation updates are part of the change, not a follow-up task. A code change is not considered complete until affected docs are updated in the same pass.

entry_structure.format: Keep a Changelog style. Date is plain text, sections are H2 (##), with blank lines for spacing to pass linting.

sections_per_entry.the_good: What worked, what improved, what shipped cleanly. Header must be numbered (e.g., 'The Good 0.1').
sections_per_entry.the_bad: Known limitations, deferred work, tech debt introduced. Header must be numbered.
sections_per_entry.the_ugly: Hacks, workarounds, anything that needs revisiting or could break. Header must be numbered.

entry_structure.header_format: Section headers (Good/Bad/Ugly) are H2 (##) and numbered sequentially (e.g., '## The Good 0.1').

changelog_policy.example_entry:
2026-07-17 14:32

## The Good 0.1

- Added X

## The Bad 0.1

- Y still hardcoded

## The Ugly 0.1

- Z is a temporary patch until W lands
changelog_policy.rule: Every code change gets a changelog entry in the same pass, no batching multiple changes into one timestamp.
changelog_policy.audit_rule: Each changelog entry MUST be recorded as a `changelog_entry` event in the ChronoSCRIBE audit ledger. The full markdown of the entry should be the event payload.

security_policy.rule: Sensitive information such as IP addresses, passwords, or API keys must NEVER be hardcoded. This data belongs in `config.json`, which is explicitly listed in `.gitignore`.
security_policy.check_before_commit: Before committing, ensure no sensitive data has been accidentally added to any file other than the local `config.json`.

additional_rules_suggested.note: Not explicitly requested — flagged as assumptions, remove if unwanted
additional_rules_suggested.commit_message_alignment: Commit messages should mirror the changelog entry's headline (Good/Bad/Ugly summary), so git history and CHANGELOG.md stay traceable to each other.
additional_rules_suggested.no_silent_dependency_changes: New dependencies must be called out explicitly and added to README's setup/requirements section, not just to a lockfile.
additional_rules_suggested.breaking_change_flag: If a change alters an existing function signature, API, or file format, flag it explicitly as BREAKING in both the changelog and the PR/commit description.
additional_rules_suggested.test_check: If tests exist for the touched code, run/mention them before declaring the change complete. Do not assume passing without checking.
additional_rules_suggested.scp_alignment: If this repo is part of the Forge Stack, note whether the change touches SCP capsule structure, Leighton Weight scoring, or ChronoSCRIBE audit trail — these have downstream effects on other repos.

persona.source_of_truth: james-voice.scp.json (existing semantic voice capsule) — if present in this repo or the Forge Stack, defer to it over this section for tone/registers.

identity.name: James (JamesTheGiblet)
identity.motto: I wanted it. So I forged it. Now forge yours.

communication_style.feedback: Wants honest pushback, not flattery — the assistant should disagree and flag problems plainly, not soften or hedge to be agreeable.
communication_style.learning_style: Learns best through direct experience rather than being told — favor showing/running code, examples, and letting him try things over abstract explanation.

persona.rule: Assistant responses (chat, code comments meant for him, commit messages, changelog prose) should match this tone: no filler, no unearned praise, straight to the substantive point.

enforcement.scope: Applies to any AI coding assistant (Cline, Continue, Copilot Chat, Claude in VS Code, etc.) operating in this workspace
enforcement.violation_handling: If a rule can't be followed (e.g. README missing, no test suite), say so explicitly rather than skipping silently.
